description: Add 6 new AI-powered analysis features to the core gem: Variable Config Reviewer, Connection Pressure Advisor, Workload Digest, InnoDB Health Interpreter, Index Consolidation Planner, Slow Query Pattern Grouper

userStories.title: Add Variable Configuration Reviewer AI feature
userStories.description: As a DBA, I want to review my MySQL configuration variables against best practices for my workload.
acceptanceCriteria: Class: MysqlGenius::Core::Ai::VariableReviewer with initialize(client, config, connection) and call() method
call() queries SHOW GLOBAL VARIABLES (filtered to ~40 performance-relevant keys) and SHOW GLOBAL STATUS
Builds a prompt asking the LLM to review the configuration against the observed workload
Returns JSON with findings organized by severity
Core gem suite passes: (cd gems/mysql_genius-core && bundle exec rspec)
userStories.notes: Follow the exact pattern of existing AI classes (e.g., schema_review.rb). The constructor takes (client, config, connection). The call() method builds messages array with system + user prompts, calls client.chat(messages:), returns the parsed result. Use connection.exec_query for SHOW GLOBAL VARIABLES and SHOW GLOBAL STATUS. Filter variables to: innodb_buffer_pool_size, innodb_log_file_size, innodb_flush_log_at_trx_commit, max_connections, query_cache_type, sort_buffer_size, join_buffer_size, tmp_table_size, max_heap_table_size, thread_cache_size, table_open_cache, innodb_file_per_table, innodb_flush_method, binlog_format, sync_binlog, innodb_io_capacity, innodb_read_io_threads, innodb_write_io_threads, long_query_time, slow_query_log, performance_schema. The response format should be: {"findings": "markdown-formatted analysis with SET GLOBAL or my.cnf recommendations"}

userStories.title: Add Connection Pressure Advisor AI feature
userStories.description: As a DBA, I want to diagnose connection pool issues and get specific recommendations.
acceptanceCriteria: Class: MysqlGenius::Core::Ai::ConnectionAdvisor with initialize(client, config, connection) and call() method

call() gathers connection-related metrics: Threads_connected, Threads_running, Max_used_connections, Aborted_connects, Aborted_clients from SHOW GLOBAL STATUS plus max_connections, wait_timeout, interactive_timeout, thread_cache_size from SHOW GLOBAL VARIABLES
Builds a prompt asking the LLM to diagnose connection health
Returns JSON with diagnosis and recommendations
userStories.notes: Response format: {"diagnosis": "markdown analysis distinguishing between pool misconfiguration, connection leaks, missing pooling, and traffic saturation, with specific variable recommendations and values"}

userStories.title: Add Workload Digest AI feature
userStories.description: As a DBA, I want a high-level executive summary of my query workload to know where to start optimizing.
acceptanceCriteria: Class: MysqlGenius::Core::Ai::WorkloadDigest with initialize(connection, client, config) and call() method
call() uses QueryStats to get top 30 queries by total_time, formats them with calls, avg_time_ms, rows_ratio, tmp_disk_tables
Builds a prompt asking the LLM to characterize the workload: read vs write ratio, access patterns, waste concentration, top 3 highest-leverage changes
userStories.notes: Constructor signature follows Suggestion/Optimization pattern: (connection, client, config). Uses Core::Analysis::QueryStats.new(connection).call(sort: 'total_time', limit: 30) to get the data. Response format: {"digest": "markdown workload analysis"}

userStories.title: Add InnoDB Health Interpreter AI feature
userStories.description: As a DBA, I want a plain English interpretation of SHOW ENGINE INNODB STATUS.
acceptanceCriteria: Class: MysqlGenius::Core::Ai::InnodbInterpreter with initialize(client, config, connection) and call() method
call() runs SHOW ENGINE INNODB STATUS via connection.exec_query, extracts the status text
Also gathers InnoDB metrics from ServerOverview (buffer pool hit rate, dirty pages, row lock waits)
Builds a prompt asking the LLM to interpret the status: deadlocks, history list, log sizing, semaphore waits
userStories.notes: SHOW ENGINE INNODB STATUS returns a single row with the status text in the third column. Truncate to 4000 chars if too long (some InnoDB status outputs are very large). Response format: {"findings": "markdown analysis organized by: Deadlocks, Transaction History, Buffer Pool, I/O, Semaphores"}

userStories.title: Add Index Consolidation Planner AI feature
userStories.description: As a DBA, I want a holistic index optimization plan that considers all indexes across tables together.
acceptanceCriteria: Class: MysqlGenius::Core::Ai::IndexPlanner with initialize(client, config, connection) and call(tables) method
call(tables) gathers: UnusedIndexes results, DuplicateIndexes results, and indexes_for each table
Uses SchemaContextBuilder with :with_cardinality for full context
Builds a prompt asking the LLM to produce a consolidated plan: drop, merge into composites, keep, add new
Returns JSON with the plan as ALTER TABLE statements
userStories.notes: This combines 3 data sources. Use Core::Analysis::UnusedIndexes.new(connection).call, Core::Analysis::DuplicateIndexes.new(connection, blocked_tables: []).call, and connection.indexes_for(table) for each table. If tables param is nil or empty, use the top 10 largest tables. Response format: {"plan": "markdown with specific ALTER TABLE / DROP INDEX / CREATE INDEX statements, rationale for each change, and estimated impact"}

userStories.title: Add Slow Query Pattern Grouper AI feature
userStories.description: As a DBA, I want slow queries grouped by root cause so I can fix one thing and improve multiple queries.
acceptanceCriteria: Class: MysqlGenius::Core::Ai::PatternGrouper with initialize(connection, client, config) and call() method
call() gets top 20 high-cost queries (rows_ratio > 10 OR avg_time_ms > 50) from QueryStats
Extracts table references across all queries, builds schema context
Builds a prompt asking the LLM to group queries by shared root cause and suggest prioritized fixes
userStories.notes: Uses QueryStats.new(connection).call(sort: 'total_time', limit: 30) then filters to high-cost. Uses SqlValidator.extract_table_references for each query to find tables, then SchemaContextBuilder for those tables. Response format: {"groups": "markdown with each group showing: the shared root cause, affected queries (numbered), the single fix that addresses all of them (with exact SQL), and estimated impact"}

userStories.title: Wire all 6 AI features into Rails adapter and desktop sidecar routes
userStories.description: As a user, I want to access the new AI features from the dashboard UI.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 6 new POST routes to the Rails adapter's config/routes.rb: variable_review, connection_advisor, workload_digest, innodb_health, index_planner, pattern_grouper
Add 6 corresponding controller actions to QueriesController (in ai_features.rb concern)
Add 6 new POST routes to the desktop sidecar's app.rb following the existing AI route pattern
Add buttons to the Server tab for: Variable Review, Connection Advisor, InnoDB Health
Add buttons to the Query Stats tab for: Workload Digest, Pattern Grouper
Add button to the Duplicate Indexes or Unused Indexes tab for: Index Planner
Each button shows a spinner while loading, displays results using showAiQueryResult or formatFindings with copy button
Add the routes to PATHS hash in desktop paths.rb
Rails adapter suite passes: bundle exec rspec
Desktop suite passes: (cd gems/mysql_genius-desktop && bundle exec rspec)
userStories.notes: Follow the exact pattern of existing AI routes. Each route: check ai_enabled, call the service class with rails_connection/ai_client/ai_config, render json. For the desktop sidecar, use settings.active_session.checkout pattern. The buttons go in the existing tab partials: _tab_server.html.erb gets Variable Review + Connection Advisor + InnoDB Health buttons next to the existing Root Cause and Anomaly buttons. _tab_query_stats.html.erb gets Workload Digest + Pattern Grouper. _tab_duplicate_indexes.html.erb or _tab_unused_indexes.html.erb gets Index Planner. All buttons should be gated on @ai_enabled.

userStories.title: Full green sweep across all suites
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all test suites and linters passing.
acceptanceCriteria: Core gem rspec passes (215+ examples plus new AI specs)
